steps.description: TURN 1: Use insert_free_model to fetch a 'sports car' from Toolbox, then use fetch_explorer_state to discover its structure. END TURN.

TURN 2: Read the Symbol Map to find the car model's path. Check its children. The model ALREADY HAS all parts/seats/scripts inside - DON'T create new objects inside it! Just configure existing parts (set Anchored, Position, etc). Set Anchored = false on ALL parts of the car model (so it can move). Position the car near the road centerline, ensuring it is at least 40 studs away from any buildings. END TURN.

Consolidate all car entry scripts into a single script to avoid redundancy and potential conflicts. Remove the redundant scripts: Workspace.CarModel.ConsolidatedEntryScript, Workspace.CarModel.CarEntry, and Workspace.CarModel.ConsolidatedEntryScript__2.
TURN 4: Verify and complete. Double-check all scripts are in the right place. Verify interaction works. DO NOT call fetch_explorer_state! Return 'COMPLETE' when done. END TURN.

Consolidate all car entry scripts into a single script to avoid redundancy and potential conflicts. Remove the redundant scripts: Workspace.CarModel.ConsolidatedEntryScript, Workspace.CarModel.CarEntry, and Workspace.CarModel.ConsolidatedEntryScript__2. Create a new consolidated script if necessary.
Remove the redundant ProximityPrompt (Workspace.CarModel.BusEntryPrompt) as the vehicle seat already has a ProximityPrompt (Workspace.CarModel.BusSeat.DrivePrompt) for entry.
